title: Hybrid Self-evolving Structured Memory for GUI Agents

raw_body:
arXiv:2603.10291v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: The remarkable progress of vision-language models (VLMs) has enabled GUI agents to interact with computers in a human-like manner. Yet real-world computer-use tasks remain difficult due to long-horizon workflows, diverse interfaces, and frequent intermediate errors. Prior work equips agents with external memory built from large collections of trajectories, but relies on flat retrieval over discrete summaries or continuous embeddings, falling short of the structured organization and self-evolving characteristics of human memory. Inspired by the brain, we propose Hybrid Self-evolving Structured Memory (HyMEM), a graph-based memory that couples discrete high-level symbolic nodes with continuous trajectory embeddings. HyMEM maintains a graph structure to support multi-hop retrieval, self-evolution via node update operations, and on-the-fly working-memory refreshing during inference. Extensive experiments show that HyMEM consistently improves open-source GUI agents, enabling 7B/8B backbones to match or surpass strong closed-source models; notably, it boosts Qwen2.5-VL-7B by +22.5% and outperforms Gemini2.5-Pro-Vision and GPT-4o.
speedrun: Researchers have introduced a new memory system called Hybrid Self-evolving Structured Memory (HyMEM) for GUI agents, enhancing their ability to perform complex tasks. Unlike previous methods, HyMEM uses a graph-based structure that combines symbolic nodes with continuous embeddings. This innovation has led to significant improvements, with some models showing a 22.5% performance boost. The development is crucial as it could make AI interactions more efficient and human-like in real-world applications.
why_it_matters: This advancement could directly benefit developers of GUI agents, making their applications more effective in handling complex user tasks.
HyMEM represents a shift in AI memory systems, suggesting a move towards more human-like processing capabilities in technology.

lenses.eli12: HyMEM is like giving an AI a more organized and adaptable brain. Instead of just remembering facts, it can now connect ideas and learn from experiences. This matters because it could make everyday tech interactions smoother and more intuitive for users.
lenses.pm: For product managers, HyMEM addresses the need for more efficient AI in handling complex workflows. It could reduce user frustration by improving task completion rates while potentially lowering development costs. This means products could become more user-friendly and competitive.
lenses.engineer: HyMEM employs a graph-based structure that integrates high-level symbolic nodes with continuous trajectory embeddings, allowing for multi-hop retrieval and dynamic memory updates. Experiments indicate that models like Qwen2.5-VL-7B show a 22.5% performance increase, outperforming leading models like Gemini2.5-Pro-Vision and GPT-4o, highlighting its effectiveness in real-world applications.
